title: pittosporum berberidoides
intro: Pittosporum berberidoides, also known as the 'Kohuhu' or 'New Zealand Pittosporum', is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum berberidoides have been associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum berberidoides can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions.
